title: elymus campestris subsp. maritimus
intro: Elymus campestris subsp. maritimus, also known as sea wildrye, is a subspecies of the Elymus campestris plant, which belongs to the grass family Poaceae. It is a perennial grass that grows in coastal areas and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. Elymus campestris subsp. maritimus has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its health benefits include reducing stress, improving sleep quality, and supporting the immune system.
